title: Egg and Jalapeno
text:
• Eggs, jalapenos, salt
• Wok

• Slice jalapenos into thin rings. Mix eggs in a bowl and add a bit of salt.
• Heat the wok and wait until it's hot. Add oil, waiting again until it's hot enough. Then add the eggs and mix a bit (like tomato and egg). Add sliced jalapenos and combine.
• Remove from heat and add salt to taste.

title: Scallion Pancakes (cōng yóu bǐng)
text:
• ~3 cups all-purpose flour
• 1 cup boiling water, 1/2 cup cold water
• 1/4 cup chopped scallions
• Salt, oil

• Mix the boiling and cold water and add flour about 1/2 a cup at a time to form a dough. Keep adding flour until the dough is not too sticky. Knead until smooth and let rest for at least 30 min.
• After letting the dough rest, knead it a bit again. Divide into 6 or 8 pieces.
• Roll out each piece of dough into a thin rectangle. Drip a bit of oil on the dough, add a bit of salt, and add some of the chopped scallions. Spread the ingredients evenly by grabbing an edge of the dough and patting around to move the ingredients across the dough. Starting from one edge of the dough, roll into a log. Then, coil the dough into a round snail shape.
• Roll the coil into a flat pancake. Heat a pan and add a bit of oil, waiting for it to get hot enough. Then add the pancake and pan-fry both sides until golden brown on both sides.

title: Pizza
text:
• 1 cup hot water, 1/2 cup cold water, 1 tsp sugar, 2.25 tsp yeast, ~3 cups flour
• Tomato sauce, pepperoni, other toppings/seasonings
• Large circle pan and standard rectangular baking sheet

• Mix hot water and cold water in a bowl. Add sugar and yeast and stir a little. Add flour gradually, about 1/2 a cup at a time, and mix until dough forms. Keep adding flour until the dough isn't too sticky anymore.
• Knead for 5-10 min until smooth. Let rise for a few hours until doubled in size.
• Preheat oven to 475°F (or whatever the max temperature of the oven is). Knead the dough a bit again, and then split with a knife into two pieces (one for the circle pan, one for the rectangle pan). Line the pans with aluminum foil and pour a bit of olive oil on the foil to prevent sticking.
• For each pan, roll the dough in olive oil to coat the surface, and spread out the oil across the foil. Then stretch the dough with your fingers, shaping it little by little, until it covers the pan.
• Add sauce and toppings, e.g. pepperoni. Sprinkle a bit of Italian seasoning and garlic powder on top.
• Bake for 18-22 min until desired crispiness.

title: Berry Pie
text:
• Frozen berries, store-bought pie crust, sugar, cornstarch
• Glass pie dish, or otherwise round cake pan

• Preheat oven to 400°F. Place one pie crust in pie pan and shape to fit. Mix frozen berries with a couple spoons of sugar and cornstarch
• Pour berries into the pie crust. Slice the top pie crust into strips and lay over the berries in a lattice pattern (weaving the strips together). Crimp the edges by pinching with your fingers.
• Bake for 30 min or 45-50 min until crust is golden brown.

• Note: We usually add much less sugar than most recipes call for, so the pie is not very sweet.

title: Shortbread Wedge Cookies
description: Note: can bake in 9x13 pan and cut into rectangles

title: Pork and Celery
text:
• Pork chops, 4 stalks celery, 3 cloves garlic

• Prepare meat:
• Cut pork chops into strips, cutting against the grain. Add an egg and mix with chopsticks to coat
• Wash celery and slice straight across into small chunks. If celery is particularly dirty, wash a second time. Chop garlic 
• Heat wok on high, wait until hot. Add a few tbsp of oil. Add garlic, couple of chilli peppers
• Add meat abd combine. Add a big spoonful of doubanjiang
• When meat is mostly cooked, add celery. Add 1.5 tsp of salt, then 2 tsp of vinegar. (Typically, people add soy sauce instead of salt and don't usually add vinegar; this is preference)
• Cover with lid on low, mixing every so often and replacing the lid

title: Chao Rou (general technique)
text:
• Prepare meat:
• Rinse meat if needed, cut into thin strips and then smaller chunks
• Add cornstarch, cooking wine, and thinly sliced ginger (strips) to the meat
• Can stir fry with different vegetables. Cabbage is very easy, just need a bit of meat. Celery, onion work well
• If celery: chop straight across or at a diagonal
• Heat oil in wok, wait until hot. Then add meat, onions/garlic. Can use lid to avoid too much smell/smoke
• Add soy sauce, vegetables. Can add a bit of water
• When ready, add salt. If desired, can add a little bit of sesame oil

title: Pidan Zhou
text:
• Add water and white rice (rice will expand)
• Boil and let simmer until the rice has expanded
• If desired, can add marinated pork that has been cut into very small pieces
• Add salt, hujiaofen (white pepper)
• Peel pidan and add to pot (can roughly slice beforehand, or inside the pot)

title: Hard-Boiled Eggs
text:
• 7 eggs

• Put bunch of eggs in pot, add water. Heat on low and wait for it to come to a rolling boil
• Turn off the heat and let the eggs sit in the hot water for a total of 8 minutes — but budget time for getting out bowls of cold water (and ice if available), so set timer for 6 or 7 minutes
• After the timer goes off, transfer the eggs into cold water. You can move them into the fridge. Eggs will be a little soft-boiled

title: Tomato and Egg Noodles
text:
• 3 tomatoes, eggs, noodles

• Peel tomatoes: 
• Cut out the bottom of the tomato with a knife, and score the top of the tomato in an X shape. Place in an inch of water and boil until the skin begins to peel. Put the tomatoes in a bowl of cold water and peel off the skin
• Stir fry tomatoes:
• Heat a pan and add oil. Add the tomatoes and smash into small pieces using a wooden spoon. Let cook until the tomato changes color and slightly reduces
• Add water, noodles, eggs, salt

title: (pre-cooked) Pork and Celery
text:
• celery, pre-cooked pork, scallions (opt.)

• Chop celery into small chunks, chop 3 cloves garlic. Slice pre-cooked pork
• Heat wok on high, check with hand that it's hot
• Add a few tablespoons of oil, 3 chilli peppers, chopped garlic. Add meat and celery, 1 tsp salt
• Add couple tbsp of pixian broad bean paste with chilli oil (similar to doubanjiang). Add couple tsp of vinegar (most people would probably use soy sauce)
• Add few tbsp of water to help cook celery, cover with lid on low heat for a bit if needed

title: (pre-cooked) Pork and Bean Sprouts
text:
• bean sprouts, pre-cooked pork, scallions (opt.)

• Wash bean sprouts, slice pre-cooked pork
• Heat wok on high, check with hand that it's hot
• Add a few tablespoons of oil, couple of chilli peppers. Add scallions 
• Order of stir fry ingredients depends on how long each needs to cook 
• First add meat, big spoon of doubanjiang (fermented black bean paste). Add bean sprouts and stir, add 1 tsp of salt
• Add few tbsp of vinegar. If desired, can add a spicy sauce like laoganma
• Can set heat to low, cover with lid for a bit if needed

title: Tomato and Egg
text:
• 3 eggs, about 3 tomatoes (depends on size), chopped scallions (opt.)

• Slice tomatoes, then cut lengthwise into strips
• Mix eggs in bowl with scallions and about 1 tsp salt
• Heat wok on high, check with hand that it's hot
• Add few tablespoons of oil to coat a portion of the bottom. Add eggs, wait a moment, mix around
• Add tomatoes, break eggs and tomatoes smaller while stirring if needed
• Add about 2 tsp sugar, 2 tsp soy sauce
• Set heat to low, cover with lid for a bit if needed. Don't let all of the juice dry out

title: Sichuan Dry Fried Green Beans (gān biān sì jì dòu)
text:
• green beans, garlic, peppercorns, bit of chilli pepper, sliced pre-cooked meat (opt.)

• Put green beans in boiling water for 90 sec
• Air fry at around 380°F for 5-6 min to dry out
• Heat oil in a pan, add aromatics (garlic, peppercorns, bit of chilli pepper)
• Then add sliced pre-cooked meat, then green beans

title: Salmon Rice Bowl
text:
• 1.5 lb salmon
• 1/2 cup soy sauce
• 1 tbsp rice wine vinegar 
• 5 cloves garlic
• 1 tbsp grated ginger
• 1 tbsp green onion
• 1/4 cup honey 
• 1 tsp sesame oil
• 1 tbsp sesame seeds
• rice, cucumber, avocado for bowl

• Cut salmon into bite-sized chunks
• Combine all sauces and dump into air fryer with tray removed
• Put salmon into bottom of air fryer and coat
• Air fry at 400°F for about 4 min
• Serve with rice, cucumber, avocado, and leftover sauce if desired

• Can use sauce with chicken, salmon, steak, shrimp, etc

title: Air Fryer Salmon
text:
• garlic powder, garlic minced (opt.), salt, brown sugar
• Want enough seasonings to coat

• Remove any scales. Slice thinner if needed.
• Spread seasonings on top of the salmon with spoon. 
• Air fry at 400°F for 6 min.

title: Air Fryer Carrots
text:
• carrots, salt, garlic powder, olive oil, Italian seasoning

• Slice into coins or sticks
• Toss with oil and seasonings
• Air fry at 400°F for about 9 min, until a bit dried out at edges

title: Air Fryer Lamb Cumin
text:
• 1/3 cup lamb, cut into 1 inch chunks
• 1-2 spoons soy sauce
• 1/2 tsp salt
• 1 chilli pepper, broken into small flakes
• 2 tsp cumin
• 1/4-½ tsp baking soda (to make softer)

• Mix all ingredients in ziplock bag and marinate for at least 30 min.
• Air fry at 400F for 5 min.
